text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A helpful method is to create SMART goals, which means they are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of a vague goal like "improve the website," a SMART goal would be "increase website traffic by 20% within three months." Once the goals are set, break the project into smaller milestones. These are important checkpoints that help you track progress. Also, define clear deliverables, such as reports or finished features. Finally, make sure all stakeholders agree on these goals. This alignment avoids confusion and keeps everyone working in the same direction, making the project more efficient.
